findings.description: The title 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' appears larger/bolder in the HTML version compared to the PDF where it matches the same font size as the rest of the header block.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' is in the same font size as surrounding header text. HTML: It appears as a larger, bolder heading.

findings.description: The centered header block in the PDF ('FACT SHEET' and the permit title/address) appears slightly differently styled in the HTML, with 'FACT SHEET' rendered as a smaller, lighter header element.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'FACT SHEET' appears in standard bold caps. HTML: 'FACT SHEET' appears as a smaller, lighter styled text at top.

findings.description: The mailing address block (Mr. Adam Ulishney, Chief...) has slightly different indentation in the HTML compared to the PDF, but the structure is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF: Address block is indented from left margin. HTML: Address block is similarly indented but with slightly different margins.

findings.description: URL in the administrative record reference is not rendered as a clickable hyperlink in the HTML, and the email address for the project manager is not hyperlinked either. However, the text itself matches the PDF, so no fabrication.
findings.evidence: PDF has URL 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/waste-management/laserfiche' and email 'caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov' as plain text; HTML also has them as plain text.

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 element. The document title 'Draft Corrective Action Permit' is rendered as an h2. There is no clear heading hierarchy — the entire document uses only a single h2 for the title with no other headings, which is acceptable for a fact sheet but the lack of h1 is a structural issue.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

Draft Corrective Action Permit

as the only heading. No h1 is present.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements — the outer <main> wraps everything, and then there are additional <main> tags inside for page content sections. This is invalid HTML structure but does not cause content duplication.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> at the top level, then two more <main> elements inside for page 1 and page 2 content.

findings.description: The contact email for Caroline Bari appears inconsistently between pages in the PDF itself (caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov on page 1 vs caroline.bari@deq.nc.gov on page 2). The HTML preserves both email addresses as they appear in the PDF, which is faithful reproduction.
findings.evidence: Page 1 PDF and HTML: 'caroline.bari@ncdenr.gov'; Page 2 PDF and HTML: 'caroline.bari@deq.nc.gov'
